blocks.text: • Systolic Pressure: Peak pressure in the arteries during the cardiac cycle.

lines.text: in the arteries during the cardiac

blocks.text: • Diastolic Pressure: Lowest pressure at the resting phase of the cardiac cycle.

lines.text: pressure at the resting phase of the

blocks.text: • Mean Arterial Pressure (MAP): Average pressure driving blood into tissues.
Key Concept: Blood flow velocity is inversely proportional to total cross-sectional area of the vessels (slowest in capillaries).
The heart acts as a functional syncytium. The electrical signal propagates through specific pathways to ensure coordinated contraction.

through specific pathways to ensure coordinated contraction.

blocks.text: 2. AV Node: Delays the signal to allow ventricular filling.

blocks.text: 3. Bundle of His: Conducts impulse to the septum.

lines.text: 3. Bundle of His: Conducts impulse to the septum.

blocks.text: 4. Purkinje Fibers: Rapidly distributes impulse through ventricular muscle.
